title: Failure Cohort Truth Playbook
description: Cut failure and NDF data into honest cohorts before enterprise panic: population age, ship date, warranty window, new vs installed base. Stop cherry-picked aggregates from driving the wrong fix and dumping cost on support.

mindset.principle: A single failure rate is a weapon. Cohort truth is the defense. Real fails on old gear and clean new gear can live in the same account.

mistakes.label: Quote one fail % for the whole account with no age cut
mistakes.cost: Product line blamed; wrong escalation; support owns chaos

mistakes.label: Sales shows only the slice that eases the customer today
mistakes.cost: Short-term calm, long-term support cost and bad vendor fights

mistakes.label: Dismiss all fails as NDF politics when silicon is actually dying
mistakes.cost: EOL fleet burns; customer trust dies for real

decision_branches.situation: Need to separate aged fleet from current shippers
Real fail signature on a defined population
Cherry-picked story already in market with customer

steps.name: Freeze the claim
steps.goal: Write the exact fail % and window sales is using

steps.name: Classify
steps.goal: EOL wear vs new-product defect vs process

steps.name: Path
steps.goal: Vendor fix, replace plan, or process; correct narrative

agent_signal.use_when: Sales or customer claims high hardware fail rate. Need cohort cuts by age/ship/warranty before product panic. Suspect cherry-picked data easing the customer.
decision_criteria: Never brief execs on a single uncut fail rate.
Split installed-base EOL from new shippers.
Real fails get vendor path; incomplete sales narratives get corrected in writing.
agent_signal.operator_context: Field pattern: enterprise server/storage (and any HW) panic rooms. Same pattern decades apart. Support owns the dig.
agent_signal.not_for: Generic job spine. Not NDF program design (see 073-078). Not legal advice.
trigger_phrases: Sales or customer claims high hardware fail rate. Need cohort cuts by age/ship/w
Need playbook: Failure Cohort Truth Playbook
